嘎.origin: 口 mouth plus 戛; the short, sharp cackling sound of a duck.

sentence.en: The duck goes quack-quack.

嘘.origin: 口 mouth plus 虚; the soft sound you blow out to say be quiet.

sentence.en: Shh, don't make a sound.

嘛.origin: 口 mouth plus 麻; a soft word added at the end of a sentence.

sentence.en: This thing is really simple, you know.

嘞.origin: 口 mouth plus 勒; a friendly word added at the end of a sentence.

sentence.en: Sure, coming right away.

嘟.origin: 口 mouth plus 都; the toot sound a horn makes.

sentence.en: The car goes toot-toot.

嘣.origin: 口 mouth plus 崩; the bang or pop sound of something bursting.

sentence.en: The balloon popped with a bang.

嘬.origin: 口 mouth plus 最; to suck or lap with the mouth.

sentence.en: The kitten is suckling milk.

嘭.origin: 口 mouth plus 彭; the loud bang of a door or crash.

sentence.en: The door slammed with a bang.

嘹.origin: 口 mouth plus 尞; a clear, ringing sound like a bell.

sentence.en: The horn sounds loud and clear.

嘻.origin: 口 mouth plus 喜 happy; the sound of a happy giggle.

sentence.en: She giggles hee-hee.

嘿.origin: 口 mouth plus 黑; the sound hey you use to call someone.

sentence.en: Hey, wait for me.

噌.origin: 口 mouth plus 曾; the quick swish sound of fast movement.

sentence.en: He shot up to his feet in a flash.

噎.origin: 口 mouth plus 壹; when food gets stuck and you choke.

sentence.en: Eating too fast can make you choke.

噔.origin: 口 mouth plus 登; the thud sound of footsteps or a thump.

sentence.en: He ran downstairs with thudding steps.

噗.origin: 口 mouth plus 菐; the puff sound of air or a giggle bursting out.

sentence.en: She burst out laughing with a pfft.

噙.origin: 口 mouth plus 禽; to hold something gently in the mouth.

sentence.en: He has tears in his eyes.

噢.origin: 口 mouth plus 奥; the sound oh you make when you understand.

sentence.en: Oh, so that's how it is.

噫.origin: 口 mouth plus 意; a little sound of surprise from the mouth.

sentence.en: Huh, what is going on here.

嚎.origin: 口 mouth plus 豪; to cry out very loudly.

sentence.en: The child wails loudly.

嚓.origin: 口 mouth plus 察; the cracking or scratching sound.

sentence.en: The branch snapped with a crack.

嚯.origin: 口 mouth plus 霍; a sound of surprise from the mouth.

sentence.en: Wow, this fish is so big.

嚷.origin: 口 mouth plus 襄; to shout out loudly with the mouth.

word.en: to shout, to make a racket

sentence.en: Don't shout so loudly.

嚼.origin: 口 mouth plus 爵; to chew food with the mouth.

sentence.en: Chew your food slowly when you eat.

囊.origin: A big bag that holds many things inside.

sentence.en: He carries a big sack on his back.

囚.origin: A person 人 shut inside a box 囗 is a prisoner.

sentence.en: The prisoner is locked up.

因.origin: Something big 大 inside a frame 囗; it points to the reason for things.

sentence.en: Because it rained, I didn't go.

团.origin: Many people and talent 才 gathered in one place 囗 form a group.

sentence.en: Everyone unites together.

囤.origin: An enclosure 囗 with 屯 inside; a basket to store grain.

sentence.en: The farmer stored up a lot of grain.

固.origin: Something old 古 inside walls 囗 stays firm and solid.

sentence.en: This bridge is very sturdy.

圈.origin: A round shape inside 囗; a circle or ring.

sentence.en: Please draw a circle.

圣.origin: Simplified from 聖; someone wise and holy who listens and speaks well.

sentence.en: He is called a sage.

圩.origin: 土 earth plus 于; an earthen dike that holds back water.

sentence.en: There is a dike beside the village.

场.origin: 土 earth plus 勿; a flat piece of open ground.

sentence.en: We play in the square.

均.origin: 土 earth plus 匀; spreading earth out so it is even and equal.

sentence.en: Divide the candy evenly.

坎.origin: 土 earth plus 欠; a dip or pit in the ground.

sentence.en: This road has a bump.

坑.origin: 土 earth plus 亢; a hole dug into the ground.

sentence.en: There is a big hole in the road.

坛.origin: 土 earth plus 云; a raised earthen platform used for ceremonies.

sentence.en: The flower bed is full of blooms.

坝.origin: 土 earth plus 贝; an earthen wall that holds back water.

sentence.en: There is a big dam on the river.

坟.origin: 土 earth plus 文; a mound of earth that is a grave.

sentence.en: There is a grave on the hill.

坠.origin: 土 earth below; something heavy falling down to the ground.

sentence.en: The apple drops from the tree.

坡.origin: 土 earth plus 皮; a slanted piece of ground, a slope.

sentence.en: The sheep graze on the hillside.

坪.origin: 土 earth plus 平 flat; a flat, level piece of ground.

sentence.en: We run on the lawn.

坯.origin: 土 earth plus 丕; soft clay or an unbaked brick made of earth.

sentence.en: The wall is built from earthen bricks.

垃.origin: 土 earth plus 立; the character for trash, used in 垃圾.

sentence.en: Please throw away the garbage.

垄.origin: 土 earth plus 龙; a raised ridge of earth in a field.

sentence.en: There are rows of ridges in the field.

型.origin: 土 earth plus 刑; a mold made of earth that shapes a model.

sentence.en: He made a model airplane.

垒.origin: Piling earth 土 up again and again to build a wall.

sentence.en: The workers are stacking stones.

垢.origin: 土 earth plus 后; dirt and grime, like bits of earth.

sentence.en: There is a lot of dirt on the hands.

垧.origin: 土 earth plus 向; an old unit for measuring land area.

sentence.en: His family has ten units of land.

垫.origin: 土 earth plus 执; something soft you put under to pad or cushion.

sentence.en: There is a cushion on the chair.
